raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a prescriptive causal mechanism and behavioral prescription as factual truth, framing sustained mental visualization as an actionable commitment to shape offspring traits",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "fetal sex is malleable after conception via maternal mental imagery",
    "consistent visualization directly determines both physical sex and moral/physical qualities",
    "the mother's internal mental state exerts causal control over embryonic/fetal development",
    "the effect requires deliberate, repeated 'impressing' of the image across gestation"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a monocausal mental-to-physical mechanism but presupposes an unexamined mind-over-matter ontology without addressing biological constraints",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the visualization practice must either produce the stated outcomes or deliver measurable ancillary benefits (e.g., maternal agency, reduced anxiety, or placebo-aligned health behaviors)",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the claimed causal effects are absent, so the statement generates false expectations, potential self-blame for undesired outcomes, or displacement of evidence-based prenatal care"
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: asserting a prescriptive causal mechanism and behavioral prescription as factual truth, framing sustained mental visualization as an actionable commitment to shape offspring traits
implicit_claims: fetal sex is malleable after conception via maternal mental imagery
consistent visualization directly determines both physical sex and moral/physical qualities
the mother's internal mental state exerts causal control over embryonic/fetal development
the effect requires deliberate, repeated 'impressing' of the image across gestation
